prompt: I need to model a rubber mount between a chassis and a subframe. The X-direction (translational) stiffness is nonlinear and follows this force-displacement table: at -10 mm displacement, -8000 N; at -5 mm, -3000 N; at 0 mm, 0 N; at 5 mm, 3000 N; at 10 mm, 8000 N. The Y and Z translational stiffness is linear at 5000 N/mm. Rotational stiffness is 200 N·mm/deg about all three axes. Write an Adams CMD script that creates both the chassis (fixed to ground) and subframe as rigid parts (10 kg each, Ixx=Iyy=Izz=1000 kg·mm² each), adds appropriate markers, and implements this elastic joint correctly.

assertions.text: A data_element create spline command defines the X stiffness table
force create direct general_force is used (Adams bushing only accepts numeric constant stiffness per DOF, not spline-based stiffness)
x_force_function uses AKISPL() to look up force from the spline based on DX displacement
y_force_function and z_force_function implement linear stiffness of -5000 N/mm
Torque functions implement rotational stiffness of 200 N·mm/deg
Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties, not part create
All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
center_of_mass_marker argument is NOT passed in any part modify mass_properties call
